theme: cramming a steamworks into one deck
title: Steamworks Deck: Cold-Rail Block Stowage
seed_problem: polyomino floorplan packing with per-block power dissipation on a grid with a cold (fixed-temperature) boundary; minimize peak steady-state temperature
mutation: composite: a pure non-overlapping polyomino packing goal is coupled to a discrete diffusion objective (integer Jacobi relaxation with a Dirichlet-zero rim) so the score is a nonlinear, superposed function of every block's placement simultaneously, not a sum of independent per-block costs; feasibility keeps the packing/rotation constraints but the objective now rewards a specific spatial ordering relative to the cold boundary rather than density or adjacency.
strategies: trivial: left-to-right shelf packing in input order at rotation 0 (reproduces the checker's own baseline B exactly)

naive greedy: sort blocks by wattage descending and place each one to MAXIMIZE its distance from already-placed blocks (spread hot sources apart, checkerboard-style); with nothing placed yet the heaviest block defaults to the deck's geometric center -- ignores the cold rim entirely

insight (strong): sort blocks by wattage descending, then scan candidate cells in rim-distance-ascending order (ties broken by angle around the deck center, walking the rim) so heavy blocks claim the closest-to-rail slots first and are spread around the perimeter rather than clumped in one corner -- collapses the 2D placement search into a 1D walk along the cold boundary, exploiting that boundary distance is the first-order term of the diffusion field
